summary: Sen. Darline Graham announced that she will run for her late brother’s seat this November, after being appointed last week to serve out the rest of Lindsey Graham’s term.

title: 5 dead after group enters Ohio river to help struggling swimmer
summary: Five people have died on a river in Ohio after several of them went into the water to help another person who was struggling to swim, authorities said Monday.

Colombian man accused of DUI at 101 mph on I-4 now faces ICE hold: PCSO
summary: A Colombian man who deputies say is in the United States illegally was arrested Saturday after allegedly driving more than 100 mph while impaired on Interstate 4 in Polk County, according to the Polk County Sheriff's Office.

title: WTA introduces gene testing for all players on women's tennis tour
summary: The Women’s Tennis Association is introducing one-off mandatory gene-testing for all players effective from Tuesday – a requirement to compete at the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics

title: Judge temporarily blocks Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery merger
summary: A federal judge on Monday temporarily blocked Paramount's proposed merger with Warner Bros. Discovery.

title: West Pasco girls softball league hosting Dixie Softball World Series
summary: West Pasco players are competing in the Dixie Softball World Series for the last time after 15 years together — including one who beat cancer at age 3.

title: People are making wildfire bets on prediction markets. Experts say it could lead to disaster
summary: Some prediction markets also allow people to bet on wildfires, as well as other climate change-fueled extreme weather events — an issue previously reported by High Country News.

title: Man arrested, charged for abusing dog in Hillsborough County: HCSO
summary: The Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office (HCSO) said a man was arrested and charged after witnesses reported seeing him abuse his dog.

title: State Department issues warning for Americans traveling or living abroad as Iran conflict escalates
summary: The State Department issued a warning on Saturday to all Americans traveling or living abroad due to "heightened tensions" in the Middle East.
